stories.headline: Aave bleeds $8.45B in 48 hours as DeFi TVL craters $13B post-KelpDAO
stories.tldr: Aave depositors yanked $8.45B in two days after attackers used stolen rsETH as collateral, dragging DeFi TVL down $13.21B.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Restaking collateral just became the contagion vector everyone feared — every cross-chain LST is now in lender risk-committee crosshairs.
the_details: Aave deposits fell $8.45B over 48 hours; total DeFi TVL slid $13.21B since the April 19 exploit.
DeFi United coalition pitched a coordinated unwind plan to absorb the 116,500 unbacked rsETH minted by the attacker.
April hack tally now sits at $606M across 12 incidents — already 79% of 2026's $770M YTD losses.

stories.headline: Bitcoin spot volume cracks below $8B — thinnest tape since October 2023
stories.tldr: BTC daily spot volume slid under $8B with price at $77,160, leaving the order book paper-thin ahead of today's Fed decision.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Drained liquidity historically front-runs violent moves — small flows can crank vol in either direction this week.
the_details: BTC printed $77,160.91 at 9:30am ET; ETH opened $2,289 and ticked up to $2,330 by 7:24am ET.
Volume is the lowest in roughly 18 months, per CoinDesk's daybook desk.
Traders are derisking ahead of the Fed meeting and an extended Strait of Hormuz closure scenario.

stories.headline: Western Union to mint USDPT stablecoin on Solana, eyes remittance flows
stories.tldr: Western Union confirmed plans to launch USDPT on Solana, pulling its multi-billion-dollar remittance corridor onto a public chain.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A 174-year-old payments incumbent picking Solana validates the chain as a TradFi settlement layer — and squeezes Circle's USDC mindshare.
the_details: USDPT will live natively on Solana, leveraging sub-second settlement for cross-border payouts.
Move arrives as Circle eyes $150B USDC supply by H2 2026, up from $112B on April 10.
Stablecoin sector hit a record $315B market cap in Q1 with $28T in transaction volume.

stories.headline: Upbit halts POL deposits and withdrawals as Polygon hard fork lands today
stories.tldr: Korea's Upbit froze POL deposits and withdrawals starting April 29 ahead of a Polygon hard fork targeting scalability and security.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A clean fork keeps Polygon's institutional pipeline on schedule — a flubbed one ices payments rails for weeks.
the_details: Upbit, Korea's largest exchange by KRW volume, paused POL transfers as a precautionary step.
Hard fork targets throughput improvements and additional consensus-layer security guarantees.
POL slid into the suspension window with altcoin sentiment still fragile (CMC Altseason index at 39/100).

stories.headline: CLARITY Act markup slips from April to May with no firm date
stories.tldr: The U.S. crypto market structure bill missed its April markup window and now lacks a confirmed date in May despite Treasury and Coinbase backing.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Every week of slippage gives the SEC-CFTC joint guidance more runway as the de facto rulebook — and pushes any U.S. token-launch unlock into Q3.
the_details: Over 100 firms including Coinbase and Ripple signed an April 23 letter pressing the Senate Banking Committee to schedule the markup.
Treasury Secretary publicly endorsed the bill earlier this month; Coinbase CEO dropped opposition.
Bill would codify the digital-commodity vs. digital-security split currently set by the March 17 SEC-CFTC release.
